concerns.text: Constitution Principle VII and FR-007 mandate 'calibrated prediction intervals via conformal prediction' for *every* yield prediction. Tasks T023 and T024 implement this only for the MPNN model. The Random Forest baseline (T020) lacks a corresponding conformal prediction task, preventing the statistical comparison of uncertainty (SC-005) and violating the 'every yield prediction' constraint. A task to wrap the baseline in conformal prediction is missing before T021 (training loop) or T024 (validation).

FR-005 requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping noise tolerance over a specific set of values. The Assumptions section lists these values as '[deferred]'. Task T029 instructs the engineer to 'resolve' these values from the Assumptions section, but since the Assumptions section contains no concrete values (only '[deferred]'), T029 cannot be executed as written. This creates a circular dependency where the task cannot proceed without an external research decision not captured in the plan, blocking the implementation of FR-005.

FR-006 and SC-002 require the entire pipeline to complete within 10 hours on a GitHub Actions free-tier runner. Task T037 ('Performance optimization: ensure 5-fold CV completes within 4 hours') is placed in the final 'Polish' phase. This ordering is unsafe; if the initial implementation of T021 exceeds the time limit, the project cannot proceed to analysis (Phase 5) or validation. Performance optimization must be a prerequisite or concurrent task during the implementation of the training loop (Phase 4), not deferred to the end.

FR-001 requires a success rate of 'at least 95%' for SMILES parsing. Task T015 logs error reasons but does not include a task to calculate the success rate and assert the 95% threshold. Without a specific task to compute this metric and fail the build or report if the threshold is missed, the requirement is not verifiable within the task flow. A task to 'Validate parsing success rate against 95% threshold' is missing.

Task T010 is marked as [US1] but includes a comment '(DEPENDS ON T005 stability; cannot run in parallel if schema is changing)'. While T005 is in Phase 2, the [P] tag on T010 implies it can run in parallel with other US1 tasks. If T010 cannot run in parallel due to schema instability, the [P] tag is misleading. Either remove [P] or clarify that it runs after T005 is finalized, ensuring the 'Producer before Consumer' flow is explicit.
